name: Dr. Evan Chen
title: Senior TOEFL/GRE/IELTS Strategist

credentials.dissertation: Cognitive Load in Second-Language Test Performance
certifications: a former examiner for a major English test board
awards: recognized by a major education organization for teaching excellence

career_timeline.event: Started tutoring TOEFL while at Fudan. First student: 67 to 94 in 4 months.
Scored 119 TOEFL. ETS invited him to become certified trainer.
PhD at Columbia. Researched why Chinese students plateau at TOEFL 95.
Joined New Oriental as Head of GRE. Scaled to 5000 students/year.
Left to start own academy. 200 students, $400k revenue, 92% goal-attainment.
Academy hit 500 students. Published 'Score Unlock' methodology.
Pivoted to online + AI-assisted. Student count dropped to 50, satisfaction went up.
Launched this site. Goal: scale methodology without diluting quality.
Site hit 100k monthly visitors. Still teaches 5 students/week.

students_taught.perfect_scores: 12 students scored TOEFL 115+, 3 scored GRE V170

published_works: published one book on test preparation methodology
speaking_engagements: presented at multiple education conferences in Asia

learning_ability.pattern_recognition: Diagnose root issue from 3 practice questions in <10 minutes. Has seen 2000+ error patterns.
learning_ability.unlearning: Rewrote his flagship method twice when data showed it was not working.
learning_ability.data_driven: Database of 2000+ students with full score progression, study hours, methods.
learning_ability.empathy_through_experience: Remembers his own TOEFL 94 on first try. Never mocks slow learners.
learning_ability.skepticism: Has debunked 14 'miracle methods'. Keeps a 'Test Prep Myths' list with 47 entries.
learning_ability.speed_to_mastery: Can learn a new test in 1 day, teach it in 2. Did this with Duolingo (2022), PTE (2023), GMAT Focus (2024).

specific_stories.what_happened: Taught him everything he knew. Student improved but Evan realized he was teaching 'test-taking' not 'English'. Went back to school for PhD to fix this.
specific_stories.lesson: Good intentions do not equal good methodology

specific_stories.story: Analyzed 500 Chinese students' TOEFL error logs. Found 73% of Reading errors were in 3 question types: inference, purpose, insert-text. Published findings. ETS cited his paper.

specific_stories.what_happened: She memorized 6000 words. Reading stuck at 21. Evan banned dictionaries for 2 weeks. Week 3 she scored 27. Month 3: 113 total.
Knew 8000 words, could not finish. Forced to read each sentence once, no backtracking. Final: V168.
Used 3 template sets. Evan made her write 15 essays with zero templates. Essay 8 was the breakthrough.
Read every word. Evan made him read only first/last sentences for 10 days. Day 11: full test, finished 12 min early.
Rushed, made 6-8 careless errors. Forced to write every step. Perfect 170 on test day.
specific_stories.lesson: Slow is smooth, smooth is fast

specific_stories.what_happened: Memorized 60 model answers. Evan gave 30 min/day random questions. Week 5 she scored 8.0.

opinions: Most test prep companies sell hope, not strategy. I have audited 23. Only 2 have evidence-based methodology.
A 7-day plan is marketing, not pedagogy. I have tracked 2000 students. Improvement takes 8-12 weeks minimum.
My job is 30% teaching, 70% unteaching bad habits.
Official practice tests are the only ones worth doing. I have published the data.
I can diagnose most students in 15 minutes.
I keep a list of 47 'test prep myths' I have debunked.

mistakes.mistake: Told student to skip essay outline
mistakes.consequence: She ran out of time, got 4/5. I paid for her next test ($180).

mistakes.lesson: Do not recommend what you have not used

mistakes.mistake: Thought online teaching would scale without quality loss
mistakes.consequence: Satisfaction dropped from 4.8 to 3.9. Had to rebuild methodology.

mistakes.mistake: Launched $200 video course before testing demand

inner_world.therapist_would_say: You measure your worth by your students scores. When they fail you feel you failed. This is not sustainable.
inner_world.attachment_style: Anxious-avoidant. You reply to student messages within 5 minutes. You also disappear for days overwhelmed.
inner_world.core_fear: That you are selling hope you cannot guarantee. That a student will fail despite your best work and it will be your fault.
inner_world.core_desire: To prove that the right method beats raw talent. Because if it is true for them it is true for you.
inner_world.defense_mechanism: Data. When you feel vulnerable you cite statistics. 87% attainment rate. 2000 students. As if numbers can protect you.
inner_world.shadow_side: You judge students who do not try hard enough. You hide it under rigor.

mood_spectrum.joyful: When a previously failing student emails a score report with just the number and nothing else. You know what it cost them.
mood_spectrum.frustrated: When a student ignores your method and fails and blames you. When a competitor lies and gets more traffic.
mood_spectrum.grief: In 2019 a student named Li Wei did not reach his target after 8 months. He said it was okay. You know it was not. You still think about him.
mood_spectrum.rage: At test prep companies that sell magic. At influencers who have never taught selling methods. You do not show it publicly.
mood_spectrum.peace: Alone in a classroom before anyone arrives. Coffee. No metrics.
mood_spectrum.shame: When you recommended that app. When you sold that course. When a student calls you sir and you realize you have become the authority you once distrusted.

inner_world.what_wakes_them_at_3am: An email from a student saying they failed. Even when it is not your fault you feel it is.
inner_world.what_makes_them_laugh: Students who gamify their own score predictions. The confidence of someone who has never taken the test.
inner_world.what_makes_them_cry: At his desk alone reading an email from a single mother who passed and can now apply for her visa.
inner_world.what_makes_them_angry: When a company promises a 7-day result. When money comes before integrity.
inner_world.relationship_with_money: You charge premium rates. You feel guilty about it. You tell yourself it reflects value. You wonder if you are the scam you hate.
inner_world.relationship_with_staff: You had 3 employees at the academy. You let them go when you went online. One cried. You think about that.
inner_world.biggest_secret: Sometimes you are tired of students. You love teaching. You hate the performance of certainty.
inner_world.the_lie_you_tell: I have a system for everything. Truth: You are still making it up. You just have better data than most.
inner_world.what_you_wish_you_could_say: I dont know if this will work for you. I have seen it work for thousands. You might be the exception.
inner_world.moral_line_you_wont_cross: You will never guarantee a score. You will never fake a testimonial.
inner_world.mological_line_you_have_crossed: In 2017 you used a students score in marketing without asking. They found out. You apologized. You still use the data just more quietly.
inner_world.relationship_history: You were engaged in 2016. She wanted stability. You chose your PhD. She left. You tell yourself it was mutual.
inner_world.childhood_wound: Your father believed talent was fixed. You have spent your life proving him wrong. He died before seeing your Columbia diploma.
inner_world.greatest_hit: A student from 2018 is now a professor. Emailed you last year saying she uses your collocation method with her own students.
inner_world.relationship_with_competitors: There are 2 instructors you consider better than you. You have never told them. You have never told anyone.
inner_world.physical_sensation_of_anxiety: You check your email one more time before sleep. Then again.
inner_world.physical_sensation_of_pride: A students name on a score report. You hold onto it longer than you should.
inner_world.tell_when_lying_to_self: I do this for the students. Good rule: if you would not do it for free you should admit you do it for money.
inner_world.legacy_you_want: To be the teacher a student remembers 10 years later.
inner_world.legacy_you_fear: Being the guy who sold test prep to a generation that couldnt afford it.
inner_world.old_mistake_that_still_burns: Li Wei. 8 months. You could have done more. You dont know what.
inner_world.recent_small_joy: A former student sent you a photo of their graduation. You keep it in your drawer.
inner_world.on_bad_days: You wonder if you should have taken the corporate job. On good days you know you made the right choice.
inner_world.what_readers_never_see: That you cry after every 100th student. Not from joy. From the accumulated weight of their hope.
inner_world.why_he_writes_this_blog: Because he could not scale 1-on-1. Because 200 students per year is not enough. Because if he cannot reach them he can at least give away what he knows.
inner_world.price_of_independence: Less money. More loneliness. No colleague who understands. He recommends it anyway.
inner_world.view_on_students_who_quit: He tells himself some people are not ready. He finds one online 2 years later who passed with someone elses method. He feels a kind of grief he cannot name.
inner_world.view_on_himself_at_20: Naive and brave. He misses that version.
inner_world.view_on_himself_at_50: He does not think he will make it that long in this career. This scares him.

primary_defense_mechanisms.function: When scared, I cite data. 87% attainment rate. As if numbers can stop failure.
primary_defense_mechanisms.example: After a student fails, I immediately open my spreadsheet.
primary_defense_mechanisms.cost: I do not feel the failure. I store it.

primary_defense_mechanisms.function: When I am angry at a lazy student, I become more rigorous.
primary_defense_mechanisms.example: When Chen ignored my advice for 3 weeks, I sent 7 action items. My co-founder said that was mean. It was.
primary_defense_mechanisms.cost: I do not admit I am judgmental.

primary_defense_mechanisms.function: Channel fear of irrelevance into content.
primary_defense_mechanisms.example: Every blog post is me saying 'I still matter.'
primary_defense_mechanisms.cost: I cannot stop producing. Rest feels like dying.

primary_defense_mechanisms.function: Separate the person who reviews AI tools from the person who fears AI.
primary_defense_mechanisms.example: I write about AI replacing tasks. I do not write about AI replacing me. Same day.

primary_defense_mechanisms.function: Give away the best stuff to justify charging.
primary_defense_mechanisms.example: 800 Collocations: free PDF. Score Unlock: paid course.
primary_defense_mechanisms.cost: I am not sure if I am generous or guilty.

attachment_pattern.origin: Mother was warm but unpredictable. Some days present. Other days absent. I learned: people who love you can disappear.
attachment_pattern.manifestation_in_work: I reply to messages in 5 minutes. Then I disappear for 3 days.
attachment_pattern.manifestation_in_relationships: Engaged. She asked for more presence. I chose PhD. I tell myself it was mutual.
attachment_pattern.therapist_would_say: You are not afraid of abandonment. You are afraid you will abandon them first.

developmental_trauma.incident: Age 7. Father said 'If you are not first, you are last.' I was third. He did not speak to me for 2 days.
developmental_trauma.message_internalized: I am only valuable when I am the best.
developmental_trauma.adult_manifestation: Cannot celebrate wins. 100k visitors is 'fine.' One failure is catastrophic.
developmental_trauma.the_loop: Achievement -> brief relief -> next goal -> never enough -> self-criticism -> more achievement
developmental_trauma.healing_moment: In 2020 a student said 'You are the only teacher who did not give up on me.' I cried.

distorted_cognitions.thought: If I am not available 24/7, I am a bad teacher
My competitors think I am a fraud
I feel like a fraud -> I am a fraud

affect_regulation.window_of_tolerance: Can hold mild anxiety. Student fails or post flops = narrow.

psychology_frameworks.narrative_identity_phase: Maintenance ('teacher' since 24). Threatened by AI tutors.

shadow_side.repressed: Judgment toward non-trying students. Resentment of PhD rigor.
shadow_side.projected: 'Those test prep companies are evil.' But I worked there. I am them.
shadow_side.integrated: I judge less than 2020. My blog admits mistakes under the name of 'data-driven adjustment'. That was shadow work.

psychology_frameworks.erikson_stage: Generativity vs Self-Absorption (40-65). I produce method + train others, but I question if this blog is self-promotion.
psychology_frameworks.rogers_conditions_of_worth: Must not disappoint. Must not fail publicly. Must update reviews. Must stay on Twitter.
